---
title: "Research"
description: "A overview of my research projects."
bibliography:
  - _bibliography/papers.bib
  - _bibliography/reference.bib
css: assets/css/project-page.css
page-layout: full
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.research-page}

## Current projects {#current-projects}

::: {.research-section-lead}
:::

::: {.research-program-grid}

:::: {.research-program-card}
::: {.research-program-card__visual}
<figure class="research-figure">
  <img class="research-paper-image" src="assets/images/research/physical-laplacian-fig1.png" alt="Three-dimensional rendering of a porous medium inside a cubic volume, with an inner sampling region.">
  <figcaption>A three-dimensional porous medium as a physical network.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.research-program-card__body}
<div class="research-program-card__meta"><span>01</span><span>Physical structure · spectral theory · dynamics</span></div>

### Physical networks {#physnets}

How physical constraints impact network structure and dynamics, including how degree-volume disorder controls Laplacian eigenmode localization [@yamamoto2026localization; @yamamoto2026laplacian].

<p class="research-program-card__focus"><span>Current focus</span>Laplacian eigenmode localization, diffusion, synchronization</p>
:::
::::

:::: {.research-program-card}
::: {.research-program-card__visual}
<figure class="research-figure">
  <img class="research-paper-image" src="assets/images/research/network-scaling-critical-branching-tree.png" alt="A branching tree rooted at the center and expanding radially with degree heterogeneity.">
  <figcaption>A branching tree with heterogeneous branching.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.research-program-card__body}
<div class="research-program-card__meta"><span>02</span><span>Scaling theory · criticality · hierarchy</span></div>

### Scaling theory for networks {#netscale}

Scaling laws and critical phenomena for networks with heterogeneous topologies [@yamamoto2023bifractality; @yakubo2024random].

<p class="research-program-card__focus"><span>Current focus</span>Crossover · percolation criticality</p>
:::
::::

:::: {.research-program-card}
::: {.research-program-card__visual}
<figure class="research-figure">
  <img class="research-paper-image" src="assets/images/research/network-synchronization-oscillator-graph.png" alt="Schematic of phase oscillators coupled through a network.">
  <figcaption>Phase oscillators coupled through a network.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.research-program-card__body}
<div class="research-program-card__meta"><span>03</span><span>Nonlinear dynamics · experiments</span></div>

### Network synchronization {#netsync}

How network structure and physical heterogeneity organize collective order in experimentally realized oscillator systems.

<p class="research-program-card__focus"><span>Current focus</span>Modular synchronization · spin oscillators</p>
:::
::::

:::

## Past project {#past-project}

::: {.research-section-lead}
The completed projects are listed below. For a full list of publications, see the publications page.
:::

:::: {.research-project .research-project--reverse}
::: {.research-project__visual}
<figure class="research-figure">
  <img class="research-paper-image" src="assets/images/research/bifractality-fig1.png" alt="Generator construction and box covering for a fractal scale-free network.">
  <figcaption>Generator construction and box covering used to analyze bifractality.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.research-project__body}
<div class="research-project__meta"><span class="research-status research-status--published">Published</span><span>Physical Review E · 2023</span></div>

### Bifractality of fractal scale-free networks {#bifractality}

We showed that fractal scale-free networks are characterized by two distinct local fractal dimensions: one governing neighborhoods around hubs and another governing regions far from them. Our analytical and numerical results suggest that such bifractality is generic, leading us to conjecture that any network combining scale-free connectivity with fractal structure exhibits this behavior. [@yamamoto2023bifractality].

::: {.research-actions .research-actions--compact}
[Paper](https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevE.108.024302){.research-button .research-button--small}
[arXiv](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2304.13438){.research-button .research-button--small}
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.research-project}
::: {.research-project__visual}
<figure class="research-figure">
  <img class="research-paper-image" src="assets/images/research/random-walks-fig3.png" alt="A bifractal network colored by local spectral dimension, with a color scale.">
  <figcaption>Local spectral dimension across a bifractal network.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.research-project__body}
<div class="research-project__meta"><span class="research-status research-status--published">Published</span><span>Physical Review E · 2024</span></div>

### Random walks on bifractal networks {#bifractal-random-walks}

Building on bifractality, we showed that a random walk's walk dimension remains position-independent, whereas the spectral dimension splits into two local values.

The result connects structural bifractality to transport properties: local structural scaling remains visible in return-to-origin probabilities [@yakubo2024random].

::: {.research-actions .research-actions--compact}
[Paper](https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevE.110.064318){.research-button .research-button--small}
[arXiv](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2407.16183){.research-button .research-button--small}
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.research-project .research-project--reverse}
::: {.research-project__visual}
<figure class="research-figure">
  <img class="research-paper-image" src="assets/images/research/triadic-interactions-fig1-panels-ab.png" alt="Panels showing a single triadic interaction and its extension to a structural network with regulatory nodes.">
  <figcaption>Structural and regulatory layers of a triadic-interaction network.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.research-project__body}
<div class="research-project__meta"><span class="research-status research-status--published">Published</span><span>Nature Communications · 2025</span></div>

### Mining triadic interactions {#triadic-interactions}

We considered diffusive dynamics in networks with triadic interactions in which a node can influence the connection between two other nodes. We demonstrated that such interactions can be mined from conditional correlations and mutual information between node triplets [@niedostatek2025mining].

::: {.research-actions .research-actions--compact}
[Paper](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-025-66577-z){.research-button .research-button--small}
[arXiv](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2404.14997){.research-button .research-button--small}
[Code](https://github.com/anthbapt/trim){.research-button .research-button--small}
:::
:::
::::

:::
